learnerValue: Precisely distinguishes specific heat capacity from latent heat and mass-independent heating.
Clearly establishes power as a rate rather than an energy total or elapsed time.
Reinforces conservation while distinguishing dissipation from destruction.
Tests the defining replenishment condition for renewable resources.
Separates current from charge and power by focusing on rate of charge flow.
Checks the resistance–current relationship while explicitly holding potential difference fixed.
Integrates the three central series-circuit rules and contrasts them with parallel rules.
Builds safety-relevant recall of the distinct live, neutral and earth-wire roles.
Tests the required practical choice of volume method for regular and irregular solids.
Distinguishes internal energy from kinetic energy alone and from heating input.
Contrasts specific latent heat with specific heat capacity using both mass and temperature conditions.
Checks the constant-temperature relationship between increased volume and decreased pressure.
Tests particle locations and charges in the basic atomic model.
Targets neutron number as the defining difference between isotopes of one element.
Combines nuclear instability, radiation emission, increasing stability and randomness.
Provides both equivalent assessable definitions of half-life.
Cleanly tests direction as the distinguishing feature of a vector.
Corrects the misconception that force or motion alone necessarily means work is done.
Tests both proportional relationships in Newton's Second Law.
Reinforces the additive structure of stopping distance.
Uses canonical examples to distinguish transverse and longitudinal waves.
Separates frequency from amplitude and wavelength.
Supports ordered retrieval of the complete electromagnetic spectrum in the specified direction.
Tests complete absorption and the absence of reflection or transmission.
Checks both like-pole and unlike-pole interactions.
Distinguishes self-produced magnetic fields from induced magnetism.
Defines a magnetic field through the force experienced in the surrounding region.
Provides concise recall of the specification's electromagnet definition.
Tests substantive knowledge of solar-system membership and hierarchy.
Links supernovae to both heavy-element production and element distribution.
Tests gravity's role as the force maintaining circular orbits.

issues: The 📈 visual cue leaks the correct answer by signalling growth or expansion when the choices explicitly contrast an expanding, contracting and unchanging universe.
learnerValue: The underlying source-backed link between red-shift, universal expansion and the Big Bang theory is otherwise accurate and useful.
